name: Yam Curry
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Vegetable oil for deep frying
1/2 tsp Chili powder (or use cayenne pepper)
4 oz Fresh tomatoes (or use 8 oz Canned tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the yams, cut them into 1-inch cubes, place in a colander and sprinkle liberally with salt. Let stand 10-15 minutes, then dry on paper towels.
2. Meanwhile, finely chop the onion, ginger, and garlic, and roughly chop the tomatoes. Heat oil over a moderate flame, add cumin seeds, and cook until they begin to splutter. Add the chopped onion, ginger, and garlic. Cook until the onion is a rich golden color. Add all the spices and season with salt to taste. Cook for a few seconds more, then add the tomatoes.
3. Heat oil for deep frying to 350°F (175°C) and cook yam cubes in batches until golden brown. Drain on absorbent kitchen paper. Set aside.
4. Add yogurt to the tomato mixture and continue cooking until the oil begins to separate out. Add water and bring to a boil. Let boil briefly, then add yam cubes, reduce heat, and simmer, covered for about 25 minutes.
5. Serve hot, garnished with chopped coriander.
Difficulty: easy to moderate
Time: 1 hour preparation, 30 minutes cooking
Precision: measure the ingredients
